title: Add retry logic + Redis session fallback to authenticateUser
author: priya-nair
body:
Implements ADR-012 in response to INC-284.

What this does:
- Caches sessions in Redis so an OAuth provider outage no longer force-logs-out active enterprise users.
- Adds bounded retry + backoff around the OAuth provider, then falls back to the cached session.
- Rotates refresh tokens on every issue to close the token-replay window Security flagged in the INC-284 post-mortem.

Why not simpler: a single OAuth call per request is what caused INC-284. Longer JWT TTLs were rejected because they widen the replay surface. See ADR-012 for the full alternatives analysis.

Risk: Redis is now load-bearing for auth. Follow-up to add Redis HA tracked in #851.

title: Introduce OAuth (Google, Azure) integration
author: marcus-lee
body: Adds OAuth login via Google and Azure providers. Replaces the basic email/password-only flow. Enterprise SSO requirement from the sales team.
